description: AN INVASION OF AMERICA By JOSEPH E COHEX From time to time the danger of Americas being invaded by a foreign foe has been presented to us by those who sell munitions of war or who have commerce in distant lands to protect or who just naturally cannot go to sleep comfortably unless the po liceman stations himself on their pavement Those of us who could be scared went around howling for pre paredness Preparedness was to have kept us out of war but it got us in Then in spite of the assurances of military and naval authorities that an invasion of America by an armed enemy is a physical impossibility there are not wanting courageous news makers and editors who discover periscopes out side their office windows and who have guns mounted on their roofs against air attacks No doubt one or two will yet be found dead in bed wearing life belts And no doubt one of the serious consequences of the forming of a sep arate peace between Germany and Rus sia would have been the racing of Hindenburgs forces across Russia and the Siberian steppes the fording of the Alaskan straits and a drive down
